message:
[MEDIUM] fcp-cbor: fix canonicalize_map_null_key assertion to match RFC 8949 bytewise ordering

The test was asserting length-first ordering (Null 0xF6 before Text "a" 0x61
0x61 because "shorter byte encoding"), which is RFC 7049 §3.9 / CTAP2 semantics.
canonicalize_map actually sorts via bytewise lex cmp of the deterministic
encodings per RFC 8949 §4.2.1 — 0x61 < 0xF6, so Text "a" sorts BEFORE Null.

The test never passed against the production sort order; `cargo test -p
fcp-cbor --lib` was red. No canonicalizer behavior change — only the
assertion and comment are corrected to match the actual (RFC 8949-compliant)
ordering the rest of the test suite already verifies.

Verified locally: cargo test -p fcp-cbor --lib canonicalize_map_null_key
passes (1/1); the fcp-cbor suite (previously 459 passed / 1 failed) is now
clean on this test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
